# TellyKeys

> A polished GTK desktop remote for Google TV and Android TV on Linux. Pair with the
> on-screen six-character code, then control the TV with D-pad buttons, app shortcuts,
> text input, an optional Bluetooth keyboard mode, and experimental voice search.
> Built first for Linux Mint and Ubuntu, with Fedora and Flatpak packaging in progress.

TellyKeys speaks the Android TV Remote Protocol v2 over the local network — the same
family of network remote protocol used by the official Google TV mobile app. ADB is not
required for normal pairing and remote control; it is only an optional fallback for some
text input and system settings shortcuts. Pairing data is stored locally in
`~/.config/tellykeys/`. No cloud account is required.

## Links

- Website: https://gaimsdevsoftware.github.io/tellykeys/
- Source code: https://github.com/GaimsDevSoftware/tellykeys
- Latest release: https://github.com/GaimsDevSoftware/tellykeys/releases/latest
- Debian package (Linux Mint / Ubuntu): https://github.com/GaimsDevSoftware/tellykeys/releases/latest/download/tellykeys_0.1.0_all.deb

## Features

- Discovers Google TV / Android TV devices on the local network.
- Pairs with the six-character code shown on the TV.
- Large remote buttons: D-pad, OK, Back, Home, Menu, volume, mute, power, and media controls.
- Editable and removable app shortcut buttons.
- Text input with a contextual YouTube search fallback when YouTube is active.
- Optional Bluetooth keyboard mode for apps that reject remote text input (confirmed working in YouTube).
- Experimental voice search that streams the Linux microphone (16-bit PCM, mono, 8000 Hz; prefers pw-record, falls back to parec).
- Tray mode and an optional Cinnamon applet.
- Built-in settings and help pages, including microphone selection.

## Platforms

- Linux Mint and Ubuntu: primary target, `.deb` package available.
- Fedora and other RPM distributions: supported from source; RPM package in progress.
- Cross-distro Flatpak: in progress.
- macOS and Windows: planned later, not yet available.

## Text input strategies

Google TV apps handle text differently, so TellyKeys tries, in order of context:

1. YouTube — opens a YouTube search URL directly when YouTube is active.
2. Bluetooth keyboard mode — sends text as a paired Bluetooth keyboard.
3. ADB — optional fallback when developer / network debugging is enabled.
4. Remote IME — Google TV remote text protocol fallback.

## Honest limitations

- Bluetooth keyboard mode may require BlueZ configuration on some systems.
- Voice search depends on the TV supporting third-party remote voice sessions and is experimental.
- App-specific text input behavior varies by Google TV app.
- RPM and Flatpak packages are not yet released; use the source install on those platforms.
- TellyKeys is version 0.1.0 and under active development.

## Keywords

Google TV remote for Linux, Android TV remote app for Linux, Linux Mint Google TV remote,
Fedora Android TV remote, send text to Google TV from Linux, Bluetooth keyboard for Google TV
on Linux, voice search Google TV Linux, YouTube voice search Google TV Linux.
